---
title: "American Revolution"
---

::: {.callout-note title="Status: draft-scored"}
This case has a provisional support rating. Full-corpus review, reliability
adjudication, and publication-grade citations remain pending.
:::

## Analytical focus

The American Revolution case examines how revolutionary rhetoric makes liberty,
self-government, founding, and collective survival sacred; how suffering and
military sacrifice authorize independence; and how tyranny and disunion are
constructed as threats to the political body.

## Explore this case

- [Corpus analysis](../../analysis/corpus-analysis.md)
- [Diachronic analysis](../../analysis/diachronic-analysis.md)
- [Metaphor-system evolution](../../analysis/cluster-evolution.md)
- [Critical metaphor analysis](../../analysis/critical-metaphor-analysis.md)
- [Rhetorical and genre analysis](../../analysis/rhetorical-genre-analysis.md)
- [Absence and agency analysis](../../analysis/absence-agency-analysis.md)

Return to [all cases](../../../../case-selection.qmd) or continue to the
[cross-case findings](../../../x-case/artifacts/qmd/x-case-overview.qmd).
